title: lora_state struct mixes atomics with non-atomics requiring different access patterns
description:
lora_l2.c:69-77 defines lora_state with mixed access semantics:

```c
static struct {
    const struct device *lora_dev;      // Protected by lora_mutex
    atomic_t running;                    // Atomic, no mutex needed
    atomic_t initialized;                // Atomic, no mutex needed
    atomic_t aborted;                    // Atomic, no mutex needed
    uint8_t eui64[8];                    // Set once under mutex, then read-only
    lichen_lora_rx_cb_t rx_callback;     // Protected by lora_mutex
    void *rx_callback_user_data;         // Protected by lora_mutex
} lora_state;
```

The comment at line 68 says 'access running under lora_mutex or use atomic read for polling' which is imprecise - it implies mutex is optional for atomics.

What goes wrong: A future maintainer must infer which fields need mutex and which don't. The TX path (lines 479-503) has a lengthy comment explaining why it's safe to read atomics without mutex - this complexity is a symptom of the mixed design.

Rewrite: Split into two separate static declarations:
```c
static struct { ... } lora_state;           // All fields protected by lora_mutex
static struct { atomic_t running, initialized, aborted; } lora_flags;  // Lock-free
```
